journey.name: Bookflow — search, basket, seats, bags & SSR
journey.description: End-to-end booking journey via the Retail API: outbound flight search, return flight search, basket creation, passenger entry, seat selection, bag selection, and payment confirmation.

steps.name: Search outbound flights
steps.description: Search for available outbound flights from LHR to JFK for a single adult passenger on a Revenue booking. Returns a sessionId and a list of flights with cabin and fare-family offers. The sessionId and a selected outbound offerId are forwarded to the basket creation step.

expected.description: 200 OK — search results returned. Contains a list of itineraries, each with one or more legs. Each leg carries its own sessionId and cabin/fare-family offers. The sessionId from the first leg and a selected offerId are forwarded to basket creation.
expected.validationNotes: Each leg's sessionId is a GUID unique to that search session and must be paired with the offerId from the same leg when creating the basket. Each offer contains an offerId (GUID), fareBasisCode, basePrice, tax, totalPrice, isRefundable, and isChangeable. Prices use GBP. Direct flights return a single-leg itinerary (connectionDurationMinutes is null).

steps.name: Search return flights
steps.description: Search for available return flights from JFK back to LHR for the same passenger on the return date. Returns a second sessionId and flight list. The sessionId and a selected return offerId are forwarded to the basket creation step alongside the outbound selections.

expected.description: 200 OK — return search results returned. Contains a list of itineraries each with one leg. The leg's sessionId is distinct from the outbound sessionId and must be paired with the return offerId when building the basket segments.
expected.validationNotes: Structure is identical to the outbound search response. Each leg's sessionId is independent from the outbound session. Caller selects one return offer and pairs it with that leg's sessionId in the basket segments array.

steps.name: Create basket
steps.description: Create a new basket containing the selected outbound and return flight offers. Each segment pairs an offerId with the sessionId from its respective search. The loyalty number is included so points can be accrued on confirmation. Returns a basketId used for all subsequent basket operations.

dataChain.source: Step 1 → random offer from search results
Step 2 → random offer from search results

expected.description: 201 Created — basket created. Returns a basketId (GUID) which is the handle for all subsequent basket operations. Location header contains the basket resource path.
expected.validationNotes: basketId is a GUID. Each segment offerId must match a valid stored offer from its corresponding search session. The basket expires after 60 minutes of inactivity.

steps.name: Get basket summary
steps.description: Reprice all offers in the basket and retrieve a pricing summary with tax line breakdowns. This call triggers the Offer MS to refresh fare amounts from live cabin occupancy and FareRule data, copies tax lines into the stored offer, and sets validated = true on each inventory entry — unblocking the confirmation step.

expected.description: 200 OK — all offers repriced. Each flight entry has validated = true and taxLines populated from the FareRule.

assertions.description: flights array must be present and non-empty.
Every flight in the summary must have validated = true after repricing.

steps.name: Get basket
steps.description: Retrieve the basket immediately after creation to verify the initial state. Confirms both flight offers are present as basket items and that the basket status is Active.

expected.description: 200 OK — basket retrieved. basketStatus is 'Active'. Both flight offers are present in basketData.flightOffers, each assigned a basketItemId (BI-1, BI-2). passengers, seats, and bags are empty arrays at this point. All amount totals are 0.00 until pricing is resolved.
expected.validationNotes: basketStatus must be 'Active'. confirmedOrderId is null until confirmed. version increments on each mutation. expiresAt is 60 minutes from creation.

steps.name: Add passengers
steps.description: Add passenger details to the basket. Each passenger record includes personal details, contact information, and an optional loyalty number for points accrual. The passengerId (PAX-1, PAX-2 …) is assigned by the caller and must be consistent across seats and bags additions.

expected.description: 200 OK — passengers saved to the basket. Response is a basket summary. Passenger type must be a valid IATA code: ADT, CHD, INF, or YTH. contacts.email and contacts.phone are required for the primary passenger.
expected.validationNotes: passengerId is caller-assigned (PAX-1, PAX-2 …) and must be referenced consistently in seats and bags. type uses IATA passenger type codes. travelDocuments is empty for domestic or when not yet collected.

steps.name: Get basket (after passengers)
steps.description: Retrieve the basket after adding passenger details to confirm the passenger record is persisted in basketData.passengers.

expected.description: 200 OK — basket retrieved. basketData.passengers now contains the added passenger record. seats and bags remain empty. version has incremented to reflect the passengers mutation.
expected.validationNotes: version must have incremented since Step 4. basketData.passengers must contain the PAX-1 record with matching givenName, surname, and type.

steps.name: Get outbound seatmap
steps.description: Retrieve the seat map for the outbound flight using the inventoryId, flightNumber, aircraftType, and cabinCode chained from the basket. Passing cabinCode restricts the response to the booked cabin only. Returns a cabin-structured map of priced seat offers with availability status. A random available seat is selected from the response and its details chained to the add-seats step.

expected.description: 200 OK — seatmap returned filtered to the booked cabin. Contains cabins each with a seats array. Each seat has a seatOfferId, seatNumber, position, price, currency, and availability status. Only seats with availability 'available' can be selected. A random available seat is picked and chained to Step 10.
expected.validationNotes: availability values: 'available', 'held', 'sold'. Seats with status other than 'available' must not be selected. price reflects the surcharge for that seat position; Business/First class seats carry no surcharge.

steps.name: Get return seatmap
steps.description: Retrieve the seat map for the return flight using the inventoryId, flightNumber, aircraftType, and cabinCode chained from the basket. Passing cabinCode restricts the response to the booked cabin only. A random available seat is selected and chained to the add-seats step.

expected.description: 200 OK — return flight seatmap returned filtered to the booked cabin. Structure identical to the outbound seatmap response. A random available seat is picked and chained to Step 10.
expected.validationNotes: Same seat selection rules apply as Step 7. The picked seat's seatOfferId, seatNumber, position, price, and currency are chained for use in Step 9.

steps.name: Add seats
steps.description: Add seat selections to the basket for each passenger on each flight segment. Seat details (seatOfferId, seatNumber, position, price, currency) are taken from the seatmap responses in Steps 7 and 8.

expected.description: 200 OK — seats saved to the basket. totalSeatAmount reflects the sum of the two selected seat prices. totalPrice updates accordingly.
expected.validationNotes: segmentId must be the inventoryId of the flight (not the offerId). seatOfferId must match a valid seat offer from the seatmap response. seatPosition valid values: Window, Middle, Aisle. One seat per passenger per segment.

steps.name: Get basket (after seats)
steps.description: Retrieve the basket after adding seats to confirm both seat records are persisted in basketData.seats and that totalSeatAmount and totalAmount reflect the seat cost.

expected.description: 200 OK — basket retrieved. basketData.seats contains both seat records. totalSeatAmount = 70.00, totalAmount = 70.00. bags is still empty.
expected.validationNotes: version must have incremented since Step 6. Both seat records must be present. totalSeatAmount = sum of seat prices (50 + 20 = 70).

steps.name: Add bags
steps.description: Add additional baggage to the basket for each passenger on each segment. Each bag record references the passengerId, segmentId (basketItemRef of the segment), the bag offer identifier, the number of additional bags, the total price, and currency.

expected.description: 200 OK — bags saved to the basket. totalBagAmount reflects the combined bag price (£180 + £180 = £360). totalPrice updates to seat + bag total (£70 + £360 = £430).
expected.validationNotes: segmentId for bags uses the basketItemRef of the segment (e.g. BI-y19a), not the offerId. additionalBags is the count of extra bags beyond the included allowance. totalBagAmount = sum of all bag prices.

steps.name: Add SSRs — wheelchair assistance
steps.description: Store wheelchair assistance (WCHR) SSR selections on the basket for PAX-1 across all segments. SSRs carry no charge; the basket total is unchanged. Returns the basketId and SSR count.

expected.description: 200 OK — SSR selections saved to the basket. totalAmount is unchanged because SSRs carry no charge (EU Regulation 1107/2006). ssrCount reflects the number of SSR items recorded (one per passenger per segment).
expected.validationNotes: ssrCode must be a valid 4-character IATA SSR code from the SSR catalogue. passengerRef must match a passengerId already in the basket. segmentRef must be the inventoryId of a flight offer in the basket. totalAmount must not change — SSRs are zero-cost.

steps.name: Confirm and pay — invalid card (Luhn failure)
steps.description: Attempt to confirm the basket with a card number that fails the Luhn check. The Payment service must reject the card details and return a 4xx error so the consumer can surface a meaningful message to the user. The basket must remain Active so payment can be retried.

expected.description: 422 Unprocessable Entity — card number fails Luhn validation. The Payment service rejects the card before authorisation is attempted. The basket remains Active and the consumer receives a structured error that can be displayed to the user.
expected.validationNotes: cardNumber 4111111111111234 fails the Luhn check and must be rejected with a 4xx (422) rather than a 500. The basket must not transition to Confirmed. The error body must contain a human-readable error message and a machine-readable error code.

assertions.description: Human-readable error message must be present so the consumer can surface it to the user.
Machine-readable error code must be present in the response body.

steps.name: Confirm and pay — valid card
steps.description: Submit payment with a card number that passes the Luhn check to confirm the basket and create the order. The basket transitions to Confirmed and an orderId is returned.

expected.description: 201 Created — booking confirmed. The basket is marked Confirmed and a new order is created. The response contains the orderId (GUID) and the human-readable bookingReference used for check-in and manage-booking flows.
expected.validationNotes: cardNumber 4111111111111111 is a standard Luhn-valid Visa test card number. payment.method valid values: CreditCard, DebitCard, PayPal, ApplePay. payment.cardNumber, payment.expiryDate, payment.cvv, and payment.cardholderName are required for card methods — passed directly to the Payment MS and never stored by the Retail API. loyaltyPointsToRedeem is null when paying by card only. On success, basketStatus on the basket resource transitions to Confirmed and confirmedOrderId is populated.

assertions.description: One e-ticket must be issued per passenger (matches the paxCount for this run).
Every e-ticket must carry a non-empty eTicketNumber in the format '932-NNNNNNNNNN'.
